pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and export_result.sh explicitly target this group name.
export_result.sh explicitly checks agent 000 group membership via API.
While the prompt asks for level 12, verifier.py grants full points (20 pts) for any level >= 10, only providing partial points (8 pts) below 10.
export_result.sh evaluates the paths against the list: ["/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow", "/etc/ssh", "/etc/audit", "/var/log/auth"]. The metadata is missing the auth.log requirement.

pi_items.metadata_value: Create critical-servers group with FIM, assign agent 000, create rule + active response
pi_items.verified_value: Create critical-servers group with FIM, assign agent 000, create rule + active response
pi_items.source: Matches the core instructions in the task description and verifier.py checks.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a 'critical-servers' group in Wazuh and assigning agent 000 to it. The configured FIM must include the paths /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, /etc/ssh, /etc/audit, and /var/log/auth, contradicting the metadata which omits the auth log. Although the prompt requests a rule level of 12 or above, the verifier logic strictly grants full points for a rule level of 10 or greater.
